---
title: "The Footy Forest"
pagetitle: "The Footy Forest | AFL Predictions"
title-block: false
toc: false
---

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(tidyverse)
library(gt)

# Read predictions CSV (overwritten each week by the R pipeline)
tips <- read_csv("data/tips_table_for_website.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

# Extract round info from seas_rnd column
round_info <- tips$seas_rnd[1]
season <- str_extract(round_info, "^\\d+")
round_num <- str_extract(round_info, "\\d+$")
n_games <- nrow(tips)
```

::: {.hero-section}
::: {style="display: flex; align-items: center;"}
![](images/logo.png){.hero-logo}

::: {}
# The Footy Forest
::: {.subtitle}
AFL match predictions powered by ensemble machine learning
:::
::: {.round-badge}
`r paste0(season, " Season — Round ", round_num)`
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.content}

::: {.section-heading}
Round `r round_num` Predictions
:::

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

# Build the GT predictions table — same style as the R pipeline output
pretty_tips <- tips %>%
  rename(
    Home = home_team,
    Away = away_team,
    "Power" = elo_differential_c,
    "Venue" = venue_name,
    "Venue effect" = venue_experience_differential_c,
    "Team rating" = team_player_rating_differential_c,
    "Travel" = travel_differential_c,
    "Predicted winner" = predicted_winner,
    "Margin" = margin,
    "Probability" = probability
  )

# If has_lineup_data column exists, set Team rating to NA where lineups unavailable
if("has_lineup_data" %in% colnames(tips)) {
  pretty_tips <- pretty_tips %>%
    mutate(`Team rating` = ifelse(tips$has_lineup_data, round(`Team rating`), NA))
}

pretty_tips %>%
  select(Home, Away, Power, `Venue effect`, `Team rating`, Travel,
         Venue, `Predicted winner`, Probability, Margin) %>%
  select(-Probability) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(
    title = md(paste0("**Footy Forest** predictions for ", season, " Round ", round_num)),
    subtitle = md("*Relative advantage columns scaled from -100 to +100*")
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Relative advantage to home team",
    columns = c("Power", "Venue effect", "Team rating", "Travel")
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md("**footyforest.com** | Powered by ensemble ML")
  ) %>%
  opt_align_table_header(align = "left") %>%
  cols_align(columns = c("Power", "Venue", "Venue effect", "Team rating",
                          "Travel", "Predicted winner", "Margin"), align = "center") %>%
  cols_align(columns = c("Home", "Away"), align = "left") %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold", size = px(13)),
    locations = cells_body(columns = c("Predicted winner"))
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(color = "#555555", size = px(11)),
    locations = cells_body(columns = c("Home", "Away"))
  ) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = c("Power", "Venue effect", "Team rating", "Travel"), decimals = 0) %>%
  sub_missing(columns = c("Team rating"), missing_text = "TBA") %>%
  data_color(
    columns = c("Power", "Venue effect", "Team rating", "Travel"),
    palette = c("#D94F4F", "#F5F5F5", "#3A8A3A"),
    domain = c(-100, 100)
  ) %>%
  cols_width(
    c("Power", "Venue effect", "Team rating", "Travel") ~ px(90),
    "Venue" ~ px(140),
    "Predicted winner" ~ px(150),
    "Margin" ~ px(70),
    c("Home", "Away") ~ px(140)
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(sides = "bottom", color = "#E0E0E0", weight = px(1)),
    locations = cells_body()
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_text(weight = "bold", size = px(11), color = "#444444"),
      cell_fill(color = "#F8F8F8"),
      cell_borders(sides = "bottom", color = "#CCCCCC", weight = px(2))
    ),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_text(weight = "bold", size = px(11), color = "#444444"),
      cell_fill(color = "#F0F0F0")
    ),
    locations = cells_column_spanners()
  ) %>%
  tab_options(
    table.font.size = px(12),
    table.font.names = c("system-ui", "-apple-system", "Segoe UI", "Roboto", "sans-serif"),
    heading.title.font.size = px(18),
    heading.subtitle.font.size = px(12),
    heading.border.bottom.color = "#CCCCCC",
    table.border.top.color = "#CCCCCC",
    table.border.bottom.color = "#CCCCCC",
    table_body.border.bottom.color = "#CCCCCC",
    column_labels.padding = px(8),
    data_row.padding = px(7),
    source_notes.font.size = px(10),
    source_notes.padding = px(8)
  )
```

::: {.section-heading}
How it works
:::

The Footy Forest uses an ensemble of machine learning models — including random forests, support vector machines, neural networks, logistic regression, and naive Bayes — stacked together to predict AFL match outcomes. Predictions are driven by four key differentials between the home and away teams: **ELO power rating**, **venue performance effect**, **team player ratings** (based on selected lineups), and **travel distance**. Each variable is scaled from -100 to +100, where positive values favour the home team.

Read more on the [About](about.qmd) page, or check current [Power Rankings](rankings.qmd).

:::
